summary: API plan for the menu module is complete and codegen-ready. All 8 cases (TC-MENU-001..008) map to concrete test functions targeting real endpoints (/api/v1/menus/{create,list,get,update,delete}) with concrete HTTP methods, the project's 'token: <jwt>' auth header convention, request bodies, and per-case assertions. Test-data plan enumerates 4 factories and a session-scoped OOB cleanup; all helpers and fixtures referenced by the plan are present on disk per Phase 6.5, and .aws/data-knowledge.yaml is fresh (workflow-state.yaml gates.data_knowledge=fresh). TC-MENU-008 is intentionally designed as a product-behavior probe of ANOMALY-002 (recursive parent_id cycle); the plan exercises the target /menus/update endpoint directly (no alternate-endpoint substitution), so this is NOT an endpoint coverage_gap by the skill's definition and known-product-issues.md is not required by the gate. Two accepted warnings: (1) TC-MENU-007 assertion shape (HTTP 404 vs HTTP 200+code!=200) is explicitly deferred to be pinned at first run, and (2) TC-MENU-008 is expected to FAIL at execution and be classified as a product issue at the inspect phase.

findings.message: TC-MENU-007 assertion shape on /menus/get after delete is left as 'HTTP 404 OR HTTP 200 with code != 200', deferring the choice to first execution. Both branches are documented (NR-API-001), but codegen will need to pin the exact branch after first run.
findings.suggestion: Codegen should implement the disjunctive assertion (status_code == 404 OR json.code != 200) and capture the actual response shape in a comment so the next iteration can tighten the assertion. No plan change needed; this is informational.

findings.message: TC-MENU-008 is intentionally expected to FAIL because the backend currently lacks a parent_id cycle guard (ANOMALY-002 in fact-baseline.json). The plan documents this via 'aspirational' assertions plus 'hard' invariant assertions (5s timeout on /list and parent_id integrity check on /get).
findings.suggestion: Codegen MUST honor the documented assertion contract: aspirational assertion uses code != 200 (will likely fail today), hard assertions use httpx per-call timeout=5.0 on /menus/list and a parent_id != self/descendant check on /menus/get. Failure should be classified as product issue at inspect, not as a test defect.

findings.message: Run guidance recommends running without -x so TC-MENU-008 expected failure does not halt other cases, but does not specify an explicit pytest marker for known-failing tests. Existing project tests do not appear to use markers, so codegen should NOT introduce one.
findings.suggestion: Codegen should follow existing project convention (no markers unless tests/api/* already uses them). Document TC-MENU-008's expected-fail behavior in a test-level docstring, not via xfail/skip.

findings.message: Plan's Blockers section (BL-API-001/002/003) reflects pre-Phase-6.5 state. All three blockers have since been resolved: tests/api/helpers/menu_api.py exists with all 11 helpers, menu factory fixtures live in tests/api/conftest.py, and .aws/data-knowledge.yaml has been refreshed (workflow-state.yaml gates.data_knowledge=fresh).
findings.suggestion: No file edit required at review time; reviewer notes the Blockers section is historical. The plan-level review treats these as resolved and does NOT reproduce them as gate blockers.
